heading: Qualification of drug development tools
label: 357 Qualification of drug development tools

Guidance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–255, div. A, title III, § 3011(b)130 Stat. 1089Dec. 13, 2016“(1).—In generalThe Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section [this note] as the ‘Secretary’) shall, in consultation with biomedical research consortia (as defined in subsection (e) of section 507 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] (as added by subsection (a)) and other interested parties through a collaborative public process, issue guidance to implement such section 507 that—21 U.S.C. 357“(A)provides a conceptual framework describing appropriate standards and scientific approaches to support the development of biomarkers delineated under the taxonomy established under paragraph (3);“(B)with respect to the qualification process under such section 507—“(i)describes the requirements that entities seeking to qualify a drug development tool under such section shall observe when engaging in such process;“(ii)outlines reasonable timeframes for the Secretary’s review of letters, qualification plans, or full qualification packages submitted under such process; and“(iii)establishes a process by which such entities or the Secretary may consult with biomedical research consortia and other individuals and entities with expert knowledge and insights that may assist the Secretary in the review of qualification plans and full qualification submissions under such section; and“(C)includes such other information as the Secretary determines appropriate.“(2).—TimingNot later than 3 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall issue draft guidance under paragraph (1) on the implementation of section 507 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] (as added by subsection (a)). The Secretary shall issue final guidance on the implementation of such section not later than 6 months after the date on which the comment period for the draft guidance closes.Dec. 13, 201621 U.S.C. 357“(3)Taxonomy.—“(A).—In generalFor purposes of informing guidance under this subsection, the Secretary shall, in consultation with biomedical research consortia and other interested parties through a collaborative public process, establish a taxonomy for the classification of biomarkers (and related scientific concepts) for use in drug development.“(B).—Public availabilityNot later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall make such taxonomy publicly available in draft form for public comment. The Secretary shall finalize the taxonomy not later than 1 year after the close of the public comment period.”

A, title III, § 3011(b)130 Stat. 1089Dec. 13, 2016“(1).—In generalThe Secretary of Health and Human Services (referred to in this section [this note] as the ‘Secretary’) shall, in consultation with biomedical research consortia (as defined in subsection (e) of section 507 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] (as added by subsection (a)) and other interested parties through a collaborative public process, issue guidance to implement such section 507 that—21 U.S.C. 357“(A)provides a conceptual framework describing appropriate standards and scientific approaches to support the development of biomarkers delineated under the taxonomy established under paragraph (3);“(B)with respect to the qualification process under such section 507—“(i)describes the requirements that entities seeking to qualify a drug development tool under such section shall observe when engaging in such process;“(ii)outlines reasonable timeframes for the Secretary’s review of letters, qualification plans, or full qualification packages submitted under such process; and“(iii)establishes a process by which such entities or the Secretary may consult with biomedical research consortia and other individuals and entities with expert knowledge and insights that may assist the Secretary in the review of qualification plans and full qualification submissions under such section; and“(C)includes such other information as the Secretary determines appropriate.“(2).—TimingNot later than 3 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall issue draft guidance under paragraph (1) on the implementation of section 507 of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act [] (as added by subsection (a)). The Secretary shall issue final guidance on the implementation of such section not later than 6 months after the date on which the comment period for the draft guidance closes.Dec. 13, 201621 U.S.C. 357“(3)Taxonomy.—“(A).—In generalFor purposes of informing guidance under this subsection, the Secretary shall, in consultation with biomedical research consortia and other interested parties through a collaborative public process, establish a taxonomy for the classification of biomarkers (and related scientific concepts) for use in drug development.“(B).—Public availabilityNot later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall make such taxonomy publicly available in draft form for public comment. The Secretary shall finalize the taxonomy not later than 1 year after the close of the public comment period.”

children.label: i
children.text: a requestor initiates such process by submitting a letter of intent to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary accepts or declines to accept such letter of intent;

children.label: i
children.text: if the Secretary accepts the letter of intent, a requestor submits a qualification plan to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary accepts or declines to accept the qualification plan; and

children.label: i
children.text: if the Secretary accepts the qualification plan, the requestor submits to the Secretary a full qualification package;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary determines whether to accept such qualification package for review; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the Secretary accepts such qualification package for review, the Secretary conducts such review in accordance with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) shall apply with respect to the treatment of a letter of intent, a qualification plan, or a full qualification package submitted under paragraph (1) (referred to in this paragraph as “qualification submissions”).

children.heading: Acceptance factors; nonacceptance
children.label: B Acceptance factors; nonacceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall determine whether to accept a qualification submission based on factors which may include the scientific merit of the qualification submission. A determination not to accept a submission under paragraph (1) shall not be construed as a final determination by the Secretary under this section regarding the qualification of a drug development tool for its proposed context of use.

children.label: i
children.text: as applicable, the severity, rarity, or prevalence of the disease or condition targeted by the drug development tool and the availability or lack of alternative treatments for such disease or condition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the identification, by the Secretary or by biomedical research consortia and other expert stakeholders, of such a drug development tool and its proposed context of use as a public health priority.

children.heading: Engagement of external experts
children.label: D Engagement of external experts
children.text: The Secretary may, for purposes of the review of qualification submissions, through the use of cooperative agreements, grants, or other appropriate mechanisms, consult with biomedical research consortia and may consider the recommendations of such consortia with respect to the review of any qualification plan submitted under paragraph (1) or the review of any full qualification package under paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: conduct a comprehensive review of a full qualification package accepted under paragraph (1)(C); and

children.label: B
children.text: determine whether the drug development tool at issue is qualified for its proposed context of use.

children.heading: Qualification
children.label: 4 Qualification
children.text: The Secretary shall determine whether a drug development tool is qualified for a proposed context of use based on the scientific merit of a full qualification package reviewed under paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A drug development tool determined to be qualified under subsection (a)(4) for a proposed context of use specified by the requestor may be used by any person in such context of use for the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Use of a drug development tool
children.label: 2 Use of a drug development tool
A
children.text: supporting or obtaining approval or licensure (as applicable) of a drug or biological product (including in accordance with ) under or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act []; orsection 356(c) of this titlesection 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262

children.label: B
children.text: supporting the investigational use of a drug or biological product under or section 351(a)(3) of the Public Health Service Act [].section 355(i) of this title42 U.S.C. 262(a)(3)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may rescind or modify a determination under this section to qualify a drug development tool if the Secretary determines that the drug development tool is not appropriate for the proposed context of use specified by the requestor. Such a determination may be based on new information that calls into question the basis for such qualification.

children.heading: Meeting for review
children.label: B Meeting for review
children.text: If the Secretary rescinds or modifies under subparagraph (A) a determination to qualify a drug development tool, the requestor involved shall, on request, be granted a meeting with the Secretary to discuss the basis of the Secretary’s decision to rescind or modify the determination before the effective date of the rescission or modification.

children.label: i
children.text: the stage of the review process applicable to the submission;

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of the most recent change in stage status;

children.label: iii
children.text: whether external scientific experts were utilized in the development of a qualification plan or the review of a full qualification package; and

children.label: iv
children.text: submissions from requestors under the qualification process under subsection (a), including any data and evidence contained in such submissions, and any updates to such submissions.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary’s formal written determinations in response to such qualification submissions.

children.label: C
children.text: Any rescissions or modifications under subsection (b)(3) of a determination to qualify a drug development tool.

children.label: D
children.text: Summary reviews that document conclusions and recommendations for determinations to qualify drug development tools under subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: all drug development tools qualified under subsection (a); and

children.label: ii
children.text: all surrogate endpoints which were the basis of approval or licensure (as applicable) of a drug or biological product (including in accordance with ) under or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [].section 356(c) of this titlesection 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262

children.heading: Relation to Trade Secrets Act
children.label: 2 Relation to Trade Secrets Act
children.text: Information made publicly available by the Secretary under paragraph (1) shall be considered a disclosure authorized by law for purposes of .section 1905 of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: any information contained in an application submitted under or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [] that is confidential commercial or trade secret information subject to or ; orsection 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262section 552(b)(4) of title 5section 1905 of title 18

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a drug development tool that may be used to support the development of a qualified countermeasure, security countermeasure, or qualified pandemic or epidemic product, as defined in sections 319F–1, 319F–2, and 319F–3, respectively, of the Public Health Service Act [, 247d–6b, 247d–6d], any information that the Secretary determines has a significant potential to affect national security.42 U.S.C. 247d–6a

children.heading: Public acknowledgment
children.label: B Public acknowledgment
children.text: In the case that the Secretary, pursuant to subparagraph (A)(ii), does not make information publicly available, the Secretary shall provide on the internet website of the Food and Drug Administration an acknowledgment of the information that has not been disclosed, pursuant to subparagraph (A)(ii).

to alter the standards of evidence under subsection (c) or (d) of , including the substantial evidence standard in such subsection (d), or under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [] (as applicable); orsection 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262

to limit the authority of the Secretary to approve or license products under this chapter or the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.], as applicable (as in effect before ).42 U.S.C. 201December 13, 2016

children.label: A
children.text: means a characteristic (such as a physiologic, pathologic, or anatomic characteristic or measurement) that is objectively measured and evaluated as an indicator of normal biologic processes, pathologic processes, or biological responses to a therapeutic intervention; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes a surrogate endpoint.

children.heading: Biomedical research consortia
children.label: 2 Biomedical research consortia
children.text: The term “biomedical research consortia” means collaborative groups that may take the form of public-private partnerships and may include government agencies, institutions of higher education (as defined in ), patient advocacy groups, industry representatives, clinical and scientific experts, and other relevant entities and individuals.section 1001(a) of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: a measurement of a patient’s symptoms, overall mental state, or the effects of a disease or condition on how the patient functions; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes a patient-reported outcome.

children.heading: Context of use
children.label: 4 Context of use
children.text: The term “context of use” means, with respect to a drug development tool, the circumstances under which the drug development tool is to be used in drug development and regulatory review.

children.label: A
children.text: a biomarker;

children.label: B
children.text: a clinical outcome assessment; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other method, material, or measure that the Secretary determines aids drug development and regulatory review for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Patient-reported outcome
children.label: 6 Patient-reported outcome
children.text: The term “patient-reported outcome” means a measurement based on a report from a patient regarding the status of the patient’s health condition without amendment or interpretation of the patient’s report by a clinician or any other person.

children.heading: Qualification
children.label: 7 Qualification
children.text: The terms “qualification” and “qualified” mean a determination by the Secretary that a drug development tool and its proposed context of use can be relied upon to have a specific interpretation and application in drug development and regulatory review under this chapter.

children.heading: Requestor
children.label: 8 Requestor
children.text: The term “requestor” means an entity or entities, including a drug sponsor or a biomedical research consortia, seeking to qualify a drug development tool for a proposed context of use under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: is known to predict clinical benefit and could be used to support traditional approval of a drug or biological product; or

children.label: B
children.text: is reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit and could be used to support the accelerated approval of a drug or biological product in accordance with .section 356(c) of this title
